essence: Answers emergency calls, gathers critical information, and dispatches police, fire, or ambulance services to incidents.

identity.title: Emergency Call Handler (999 / Control Room Operator)
identity.summary: Receives emergency calls, assesses situations, provides immediate guidance, and coordinates the dispatch of emergency services.

salary.notes: Supervisors, senior controllers, and specialist dispatchers can exceed £40k–£50k+.

qualifications: Emergency call handling or control room training
Ability to remain calm under pressure
Understanding of emergency service protocols and triage systems

lifestyle.description: Emergency call handlers work in control rooms answering 999 calls, gathering essential details, providing immediate advice, and dispatching emergency services. The role involves shift work, high‑pressure decision‑making, and constant communication. It suits individuals who stay calm under stress, think quickly, and want to make a direct impact on public safety.

habits: Listens actively and asks precise questions
Follows structured triage and dispatch protocols
Stays calm and reassuring during emergencies
